<content>1: # TokMan 🌸
2: 
3: **Token-Aware CLI Proxy** — Reduce token usage in LLM interactions by filtering verbose command output.
4: 
5: TokMan intercepts CLI commands, filters their output, and tracks token savings in a SQLite database.
6: 
7: ## Features
8: 
9: - 🔧 **Git Command Wrappers** — Filtered `status`, `diff`, `log`, `add`, `commit`, `push`, `pull`, and more
10: - 📁 **LS Handler** — Hide noise directories (.git, node_modules, target, etc.)
11: - 🐳 **Infrastructure Wrappers** — Docker, kubectl, AWS CLI with filtered output
12: - 📦 **Package Managers** — npm, pnpm, pip, cargo with compact output
13: - 🧪 **Test Runners** — Go, pytest, vitest, jest, npm test, playwright with aggregated results
14: - 🔨 **Build Tools** — Go, cargo, next.js with error-only output
15: - 📊 **Token Tracking** — SQLite-based metrics on tokens saved
16: - 🔄 **Shell Integration** — Automatic command rewriting via shell hooks
17: - 🔐 **Integrity Verification** — SHA-256 hook verification for security
18: - 💰 **Economics Analysis** — Compare spending vs savings with quota estimates
19: - 💾 **Tee on Failure** — Auto-saves full output when commands fail for debugging
20: 
21: ## Installation
22: 
23: ```bash
24: # Clone and build
25: git clone https://github.com/GrayCodeAI/tokman.git
26: cd tokman
27: go build -o tokman ./cmd/tokman
28: 
29: # Install to PATH (optional)
30: sudo mv tokman /usr/local/bin/
31: ```
32: 
33: ### Docker
34: 
35: ```bash
36: # Pull from GitHub Container Registry (once published)
37: docker pull ghcr.io/graycodeai/tokman:latest
38: 
39: # Or build locally
40: docker build -f docker/Dockerfile -t tokman:latest .

42: # Run with dashboard
43: docker run -d -p 8080:8080 -v tokman-data:/home/tokman/.local/share/tokman tokman:latest

45: # Run with docker-compose
46: cd docker && docker-compose up -d

180: 
181: ## Shell Integration
182: 
183: ### Token Counting (tiktoken)
184: 
185: TokMan includes direct token counting using OpenAI's tiktoken tokenizer:
186: 
187: ```bash
188: # Count tokens in text
189: tokman count "Hello, world!"
190: 
191: # Count tokens from stdin
192: cat file.txt | tokman count
193: 
194: # Count tokens in a file
195: tokman count file.go
196: 
197: # Use specific model encoding
198: tokman count --model gpt-4o "Hello, world!"
199: tokman count --model claude-3-sonnet "Hello, world!"
200: 
201: # Compare heuristic vs actual count
202: tokman count --compare "Your text here"
203: 
204: # Count multiple files
205: tokman count --files *.go
206: ```
207: 
208: Supported encodings:
209: - `cl100k_base` — GPT-4, GPT-3.5-turbo, Claude, text-embedding-ada-002
210: - `o200k_base` — GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini
211: - `p50k_base` — GPT-3 (davinci, curie, babbage, ada)
212: 
213: Add to your `.bashrc` or `.zshrc`:
214: 
215: ```bash
216: source /path/to/tokman/hooks/tokman-rewrite.sh
217: ```
218: 
219: This enables:
220: - Automatic command rewriting for all supported commands
221: - `ts` — alias for `tokman status`
222: - `tr` — alias for `tokman rewrite`
223: - `tokman_install_hook` — install hook to shell config
224: - `tokman_status` — show integration status
225: 
226: ### Shell Completions
227: 
228: Enable autocompletions for your shell:
229: 
230: ```bash
231: # Bash
232: source <(tokman completion bash)
233: 
234: # Zsh
235: source <(tokman completion zsh)
236: 
237: # Fish
238: tokman completion fish | source
239: ```
240: 
241: Or save the completion files from `completions/` directory.
242: 
243: ## Custom Filter Plugins
244: 
245: Create JSON-based filter plugins in `~/.config/tokman/plugins/`:
246: 
247: ```json
248: {
249:   "name": "hide-npm-warnings",

251:   "enabled": true,
252:   "patterns": ["npm WARN deprecated"],
253:   "mode": "hide"
254: }
255: ```
256: 
257: ```bash
258: # Plugin management
259: tokman plugin list           # List loaded plugins
260: tokman plugin create myfilter # Create new plugin template
261: tokman plugin enable myfilter # Enable a plugin
262: tokman plugin disable myfilter # Disable a plugin
263: tokman plugin examples       # Generate example plugins
264: ```
265: 
266: ## Web Dashboard
267: 
268: Launch an interactive dashboard to visualize token savings:
269: 
270: ```bash
271: # Start dashboard on default port (8080)
272: tokman dashboard
273: 
274: # Custom port
275: tokman dashboard --port 3000

279: ```
280: 
281: Features:
282: - Real-time token savings charts
283: - Daily/weekly/monthly breakdowns
284: - Command-level analytics
285: - Cost tracking with Claude API rates
286: - RESTful API endpoints (`/api/stats`, `/api/daily`, `/api/commands`)
287: 
288: ## CI/CD Integration
289: 
290: Include TokMan in your CI pipelines for automated reporting:
291: 
292: ### GitHub Actions
293: 
294: ```yaml
295: # .github/workflows/tokman.yml
296: name: Token Savings Report
297: on: [workflow_run]
298: 
299: jobs:
300:   report:
301:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
302:     steps:
303:       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
304:       - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
305:         with: { go-version: '1.21' }
306:       - run: go install github.com/GrayCodeAI/tokman/cmd/tokman@latest
307:       - run: tokman summary --format markdown >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
308: ```
309: 
310: See `templates/` for complete GitHub Actions and GitLab CI examples.
311: 
312: ## Configuration
313: 
314: Config file: `~/.config/tokman/config.toml`
315: 
316: ```toml
317: [tracking]
318: enabled = true
319: telemetry = false
320: 
321: [filter]
322: mode = "minimal"  # "minimal" or "aggressive"
323: noise_dirs = [
324:     ".git", "node_modules", "target",
325:     "__pycache__", ".venv", "vendor",
326:     ".idea", ".vscode", "dist", "build"
327: ]
328: 
329: [hooks]
330: excluded_commands = []
331: ```
332: 
333: ## Gain Command Features
334: 
335: The `tokman gain` command provides comprehensive savings analysis:
336: 
337: ```bash
338: # Basic summary
339: tokman gain
340: 
341: # Show ASCII graph of daily savings
342: tokman gain --graph
343: 
344: # Show recent command history
345: tokman gain --history
346: 
347: # Show quota analysis (subscription tier estimate)
348: tokman gain --quota --tier pro
349: 
350: # Time breakdowns
351: tokman gain --daily
352: tokman gain --weekly
353: tokman gain --monthly
354: tokman gain --all
355: 
356: # Export data
357: tokman gain --format json
358: tokman gain --format csv
359: 
360: # Filter to current project
361: tokman gain --project
362: ```
363: 
364: ## Security: Integrity Verification
365: 
366: TokMan protects against hook tampering:
367: 
368: ```bash
369: # Verify hook integrity
370: tokman verify
371: 
372: # Runtime checks are automatic for operational commands
373: # Hooks are verified via SHA-256 hash stored during init
374: ```
375: 
376: ## Architecture
377: 
378: ```
379: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
380: │                    TokMan CLI                   │
381: ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
382: │  Command Handlers (internal/commands/)          │
383: │  ├── git.go       - Git wrappers               │
384: │  ├── docker.go    - Docker filtering           │
385: │  ├── go.go        - Go build/test              │
386: │  ├── cargo.go     - Rust commands              │
387: │  ├── npm.go       - npm/pnpm/npx               │
388: │  ├── pytest.go    - Python tests               │
389: │  ├── gain.go      - Savings analysis           │
390: │  ├── economics.go - Cost analysis              │
391: │  └── ...          - 40+ command handlers       │
392: ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
393: │  Core Engine (internal/)                        │
394: │  ├── filter/      - Output filtering            │
395: │  ├── tracking/    - SQLite token tracking       │
396: │  ├── config/      - TOML config loader          │
397: │  ├── integrity/   - SHA-256 hook verification   │
398: │  └── economics/   - Cost analysis engine        │
399: ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
400: │  Shell Integration (hooks/)                     │
401: │  └── tokman-rewrite.sh - Bash/Zsh hook         │
402: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
403: ```
404: 
405: ## Development
406: 
407: ```bash
408: # Run tests
409: go test ./...
410: 
411: # Build
412: go build -o tokman ./cmd/tokman
413: 
414: # Run with verbose output
415: ./tokman -v git status
416: ```
417: 
418: ## Token Estimation
419: 
420: TokMan uses a simple heuristic for token estimation:
421: 
422: ```go
423: tokens = ceil(text.length / 4.0)
424: ```
425: 
426: This provides a reasonable approximation for tracking savings without requiring an actual tokenizer.
427: 
428: ## Data Storage
429: 
430: Following XDG Base Directory Specification:
431: 
432: | Resource | Path |
433: |----------|------|
434: | Config | `~/.config/tokman/config.toml` |
435: | Database | `~/.config/tokman/tracking.db` |

13: // Encoding represents a tokenizer encoding type.
14: type Encoding string
15: 
16: const (
17: 	// Cl100kBase is the encoding for GPT-4, GPT-3.5-turbo, text-embedding-ada-002.
18: 	Cl100kBase Encoding = "cl100k_base"
19: 	// O200kBase is the encoding for GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini.
20: 	O200kBase Encoding = "o200k_base"
21: 	// P50kBase is the encoding for GPT-3 (davinci, curie, babbage, ada).
22: 	P50kBase Encoding = "p50k_base"
23: 	// R50kBase is the encoding for GPT-3 (davinci, curie, babbage, ada) without regex splitting.
24: 	R50kBase Encoding = "r50k_base"
25: )
26: 
27: // ModelToEncoding maps model names to their encodings.
28: var ModelToEncoding = map[string]Encoding{
29: 	// GPT-4o family
30: 	"gpt-4o":                 O200kBase,
31: 	"gpt-4o-mini":            O200kBase,
32: 	"gpt-4o-2024-05-13":      O200kBase,
33: 	"gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18": O200kBase,
34: 	// GPT-4 family
35: 	"gpt-4":               Cl100kBase,
36: 	"gpt-4-turbo":         Cl100kBase,
37: 	"gpt-4-turbo-preview": Cl100kBase,
38: 	"gpt-4-0125-preview":  Cl100kBase,
39: 	"gpt-4-1106-preview":  Cl100kBase,
40: 	"gpt-4-0613":          Cl100kBase,
41: 	"gpt-4-0314":          Cl100kBase,
42: 	// GPT-3.5 family
43: 	"gpt-3.5-turbo":      Cl100kBase,
44: 	"gpt-3.5-turbo-0125": Cl100kBase,
45: 	"gpt-3.5-turbo-1106": Cl100kBase,
46: 	"gpt-3.5-turbo-0613": Cl100kBase,
47: 	"gpt-3.5-turbo-0301": Cl100kBase,
48: 	// Embedding models
49: 	"text-embedding-ada-002": Cl100kBase,
50: 	"text-embedding-3-small": Cl100kBase,
51: 	"text-embedding-3-large": Cl100kBase,
52: 	// Legacy GPT-3
53: 	"davinci": P50kBase,
54: 	"curie":   P50kBase,
55: 	"babbage": P50kBase,
56: 	"ada":     P50kBase,
57: 	// Claude (approximation - uses similar tokenization)
58: 	"claude-3-opus":     Cl100kBase,
59: 	"claude-3-sonnet":   Cl100kBase,
60: 	"claude-3-haiku":    Cl100kBase,
61: 	"claude-3.5-sonnet": Cl100kBase,
62: 	"claude-3.5-haiku":  Cl100kBase,
63: }
64: 
65: // Tokenizer wraps the tiktoken tokenizer.
66: type Tokenizer struct {
67: 	codec   tiktoken.Codec
68: 	encName Encoding
69: }
70: 
71: // New creates a new Tokenizer with the specified encoding.
72: func New(enc Encoding) (*Tokenizer, error) {
73: 	codec, err := tiktoken.Get(tiktoken.Encoding(enc))
74: 	if err != nil {
75: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get encoding %s: %w", enc, err)
76: 	}
77: 
78: 	return &Tokenizer{
79: 		codec:   codec,
80: 		encName: enc,
81: 	}, nil
82: }
83: 
84: // NewForModel creates a Tokenizer for a specific model.
85: func NewForModel(model string) (*Tokenizer, error) {
86: 	enc, ok := ModelToEncoding[model]
87: 	if !ok {
88: 		// Default to cl100k_base for unknown models
89: 		enc = Cl100kBase
90: 	}
91: 	return New(enc)
92: }
93: 
94: // Count returns the number of tokens in the given text.
95: func (t *Tokenizer) Count(text string) int {
96: 	if text == "" {
97: 		return 0
98: 	}
99: 	count, err := t.codec.Count(text)
100: 	if err != nil {
101: 		return EstimateTokens(text)
102: 	}
103: 	return count
104: }
105: 
106: // CountWithDetails returns token count and tokens.
107: func (t *Tokenizer) CountWithDetails(text string) (int, []string) {
108: 	if text == "" {
109: 		return 0, nil
110: 	}
111: 	ids, tokens, err := t.codec.Encode(text)
112: 	if err != nil {
113: 		return EstimateTokens(text), nil
114: 	}
115: 	return len(ids), tokens
116: }
117: 
118: // EncodingName returns the encoding name.
119: func (t *Tokenizer) EncodingName() Encoding {
120: 	return t.encName
121: }
122: 
123: // CountFile counts tokens in a file.
124: func (t *Tokenizer) CountFile(path string) (int, error) {
125: 	file, err := os.Open(path)
126: 	if err != nil {
127: 		return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to open file: %w", err)
128: 	}
129: 	defer file.Close()
130: 
131: 	return t.CountReader(file)
132: }
133: 
134: // CountReader counts tokens from an io.Reader.
135: func (t *Tokenizer) CountReader(r io.Reader) (int, error) {
136: 	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(r)
137: 	total := 0
138: 
139: 	for scanner.Scan() {
140: 		line := scanner.Text()
141: 		total += t.Count(line)
142: 		// Add 1 for the newline character (models see newlines as tokens)
143: 		total += 1
144: 	}
145: 
146: 	if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
147: 		return 0, fmt.Errorf("error reading: %w", err)
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	return total, nil
151: }
152: 
153: // CountMessages counts tokens in OpenAI chat message format.
154: func (t *Tokenizer) CountMessages(messages []Message) int {
155: 	// Every message follows <im_start>{role/name}\n{content}<im_end>\n
156: 	// This adds approximately 4 tokens per message
157: 	tokensPerMessage := 4
158: 
159: 	total := 0
160: 	for _, msg := range messages {
161: 		total += tokensPerMessage
162: 		total += t.Count(msg.Role)
163: 		total += t.Count(msg.Content)
164: 		if msg.Name != "" {
165: 			total += t.Count(msg.Name)
166: 			// Name field adds 1 token
167: 			total += 1
168: 		}
169: 	}
170: 	// Every reply is primed with <im_start>assistant
171: 	total += 3
172: 	return total
173: }
174: 
175: // Message represents a chat message.
176: type Message struct {
177: 	Role    string `json:"role"`
178: 	Content string `json:"content"`
179: 	Name    string `json:"name,omitempty"`
180: }
181: 
182: // EstimateTokens provides a quick heuristic token count.
183: // Uses the formula: ceil(text.length / 4.0)
184: // This is a fallback when tiktoken is not needed.
185: func EstimateTokens(text string) int {
186: 	return (len(text) + 3) / 4
187: }
188: 
189: // CompareCounts compares heuristic vs actual token count.
190: func CompareCounts(text string) (heuristic, actual int, diff float64) {
191: 	heuristic = EstimateTokens(text)
192: 
193: 	t, err := New(Cl100kBase)
194: 	if err != nil {
195: 		return heuristic, heuristic, 0
196: 	}
197: 
198: 	actual = t.Count(text)
199: 
200: 	if actual > 0 {
201: 		diff = float64(heuristic-actual) / float64(actual) * 100
202: 	}
203: 
204: 	return
205: }
206: 
207: // FormatCount formats a token count with model context limits.
208: func FormatCount(count int, model string) string {
209: 	var limit int
210: 	switch {
211: 	case strings.HasPrefix(model, "gpt-4o") || strings.HasPrefix(model, "claude-3"):
212: 		limit = 128000
213: 	case strings.HasPrefix(model, "gpt-4"):
214: 		limit = 128000
215: 	case strings.HasPrefix(model, "gpt-3.5"):
216: 		limit = 16385
217: 	default:
218: 		limit = 8192
219: 	}
220: 
221: 	pct := float64(count) / float64(limit) * 100
222: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%d tokens (%.1f%% of %d context)", count, pct, limit)
223: }
224: 
225: // CountStats holds statistics about token counting.
226: type CountStats struct {
227: 	TotalTokens int
228: 	TotalChars  int
229: 	TotalLines  int
230: 	FilesCount  int
231: 	Encoding    Encoding
232: }
233: 
234: // Summary returns a formatted summary of the stats.
235: func (s *CountStats) Summary() string {
236: 	var b strings.Builder
237: 	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "📊 Token Count Summary\n")
238: 	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "────────────────────────────────────\n")
239: 	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Encoding: %s\n", s.Encoding)
240: 	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Tokens:   %d\n", s.TotalTokens)
241: 	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Chars:    %d\n", s.TotalChars)
242: 	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Lines:    %d\n", s.TotalLines)
243: 	if s.FilesCount > 0 {
244: 		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Files:    %d\n", s.FilesCount)
245: 	}
246: 	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "────────────────────────────────────\n")
247: 	if s.TotalChars > 0 {
248: 		ratio := float64(s.TotalTokens) / float64(s.TotalChars)
249: 		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Token/Char ratio: %.3f\n", ratio)
250: 	}
251: 	return b.String()
252: }

31: 	LangUnknown    Language = "unknown"
32: )
33: 
34: // Filter defines the interface for output filters.
35: type Filter interface {
36: 	// Name returns the filter name.
37: 	Name() string
38: 	// Apply processes the input and returns filtered output with tokens saved.
39: 	Apply(input string, mode Mode) (output string, tokensSaved int)
40: }
41: 
42: // Engine combines multiple filters.
43: type Engine struct {
44: 	filters []Filter
45: 	mode    Mode
46: }
47: 
48: // NewEngine creates a new filter engine with all registered filters.
49: func NewEngine(mode Mode) *Engine {
50: 	filters := []Filter{
51: 		NewANSIFilter(),
52: 		NewCommentFilter(),
53: 		NewImportFilter(),
54: 		NewLogAggregator(),
55: 	}
56: 	if mode == ModeAggressive {
57: 		filters = append(filters, NewBodyFilter())
58: 	}
59: 
60: 	return &Engine{
61: 		filters: filters,
62: 		mode:    mode,
63: 	}
64: }
65: 
66: // Process applies all filters to the input.
67: func (e *Engine) Process(input string) (string, int) {
68: 	output := input
69: 	totalSaved := 0
70: 
71: 	for _, filter := range e.filters {
72: 		// Skip body filter in minimal mode
73: 		if e.mode == ModeMinimal && filter.Name() == "body" {
74: 			continue
75: 		}
76: 
77: 		filtered, saved := filter.Apply(output, e.mode)
78: 		output = filtered
79: 		totalSaved += saved
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	return output, totalSaved
83: }
84: 
85: // ModeNone = raw passthrough
86: func (e *Engine) ProcessWithLang(input string, lang string) (string, int) {
87: 	// Language-specific processing can be added here
88: 	return e.Process(input)
89: }

91: // DetectLanguageFromInput detects language from input content
92: func DetectLanguageFromInput(input string) Language {
93: 	if strings.Contains(input, "package ") || strings.Contains(input, "func ") {
94: 		return LangGo
95: 	}
96: 	if strings.Contains(input, "fn ") || strings.Contains(input, "pub fn") {
97: 		return LangRust
98: 	}
99: 	if strings.Contains(input, "def ") || strings.Contains(input, "class ") {
100: 		if strings.Contains(input, ":") && !strings.Contains(input, "{") {
101: 			return LangPython
102: 		}
103: 		if strings.Contains(input, "import ") {
104: 			return LangPython
105: 		}
106: 	}
107: 	if strings.Contains(input, "function ") || strings.Contains(input, "const ") {
108: 		if strings.Contains(input, ":") {
109: 			return LangTypeScript
110: 		}
111: 		return LangJavaScript
112: 	}
113: 	if strings.Contains(input, "SELECT") || strings.Contains(input, "FROM") || strings.Contains(input, "WHERE") || strings.Contains(input, "INSERT") || strings.Contains(input, "UPDATE") {
114: 		return LangSQL
115: 	}
116: 	return LangUnknown
117: }
118: 
119: // SetMode changes the filter mode.
120: func (e *Engine) SetMode(mode Mode) {
121: 	e.mode = mode
122: }
123: 
124: // EstimateTokens provides a heuristic token count.
125: // Uses the formula: ceil(text.length / 4.0)
126: func EstimateTokens(text string) int {
127: 	return (len(text) + 3) / 4
128: }
129: 
130: // CalculateTokensSaved computes token savings between original and filtered.
131: func CalculateTokensSaved(original, filtered string) int {
132: 	originalTokens := EstimateTokens(original)
133: 	filteredTokens := EstimateTokens(filtered)
134: 	if originalTokens > filteredTokens {
135: 		return originalTokens - filteredTokens
136: 	}
137: 	return 0
138: }

140: // IsCode checks if the output looks like source code.

142: 	codeIndicators := []string{
143: 		"func ", "function ", "def ", "class ", "struct ",
144: 		"import ", "package ", "use ", "require(",
145: 		"pub fn", "pub struct", "pub async",
146: 		"//", "/*", "#!", "package main",
147: 	}
148: 
149: 	for _, indicator := range codeIndicators {
150: 		if strings.Contains(output, indicator) {
151: 			return true
152: 		}
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	return false
156: }
157: 
158: // DetectLanguage attempts to detect the programming language from output.
159: func DetectLanguage(output string) string {
160: 	if strings.Contains(output, "package ") || strings.Contains(output, "func ") {
161: 		return "go"
162: 	}
163: 	if strings.Contains(output, "fn ") || strings.Contains(output, "pub fn") {
164: 		return "rust"
165: 	}
166: 	if strings.Contains(output, "def ") || strings.Contains(output, "import ") {
167: 		if strings.Contains(output, ":") && !strings.Contains(output, "{") {
168: 			return "python"
169: 		}
170: 	}
171: 	if strings.Contains(output, "function ") || strings.Contains(output, "const ") {
172: 		return "javascript"
173: 	}
174: 	return "unknown"
175: }

43: 	output = csiPattern.ReplaceAllString(output, "")
44: 	output = sgrPattern.ReplaceAllString(output, "")

3: import (
4: 	"regexp"

9: // LogAggregator deduplicates and compresses log output.
10: type LogAggregator struct {
11: 	timestampPatterns []*regexp.Regexp
12: }
13: 
14: // NewLogAggregator creates a new log aggregator.
15: func NewLogAggregator() *LogAggregator {
16: 	return &LogAggregator{
17: 		timestampPatterns: LogTimestampPatterns,
18: 	}
19: }
20: 
21: // Name returns the filter name.
22: func (f *LogAggregator) Name() string {
23: 	return "aggregator"
24: }
25: 
26: // Apply aggregates log output and returns token savings.
27: func (f *LogAggregator) Apply(input string, mode Mode) (string, int) {
28: 	original := len(input)
29: 
30: 	// Check if this looks like log output
31: 	if !f.isLogOutput(input) {
32: 		return input, 0
33: 	}
34: 
35: 	// Apply aggregations
36: 	output := f.aggregate(input)
37: 
38: 	bytesSaved := original - len(output)
39: 	tokensSaved := bytesSaved / 4
40: 
41: 	return output, tokensSaved
42: }
43: 
44: // isLogOutput checks if the output looks like log/terminal output.
45: func (f *LogAggregator) isLogOutput(input string) bool {
46: 	lines := strings.Split(input, "\n")
47: 	if len(lines) < 3 {
48: 		return false
49: 	}
50: 
51: 	// Check for log patterns
52: 	logIndicators := 0
53: 	for _, line := range lines {
54: 		for _, pattern := range f.timestampPatterns {
55: 			if pattern.MatchString(line) {
56: 				logIndicators++
57: 				break
58: 			}
59: 		}
60: 	}
61: 
62: 	// If more than 20% of lines have timestamps, it's likely log output
63: 	return float64(logIndicators)/float64(len(lines)) > 0.2
64: }
65: 
66: // aggregate applies all aggregation strategies.
67: func (f *LogAggregator) aggregate(input string) string {
68: 	output := f.deduplicateLines(input)
69: 	output = f.aggregateTestResults(output)
70: 	output = f.aggregateBuildOutput(output)
71: 	return output
72: }
73: 
74: // deduplicateLines collapses consecutive duplicate lines.
75: // Example: 10 lines of "error: connection refused"
76: //
77: //	→ "error: connection refused (x10)"
78: func (f *LogAggregator) deduplicateLines(input string) string {
79: 	lines := strings.Split(input, "\n")
80: 	if len(lines) == 0 {
81: 		return input
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	var result []string
85: 	var prev string
86: 	count := 1
87: 
88: 	for i, line := range lines {
89: 		// Strip timestamps for comparison
90: 		normalized := f.stripTimestamp(line)
91: 
92: 		if normalized == prev && normalized != "" {
93: 			count++
94: 		} else {
95: 			// Output previous line with count
96: 			if prev != "" {
97: 				if count > 1 {
98: 					result = append(result, prev+" (x"+strconv.Itoa(count)+")")
99: 				} else {
100: 					result = append(result, prev)
101: 				}
102: 			}
103: 			prev = line
104: 			count = 1
105: 		}
106: 
107: 		// Handle last line
108: 		if i == len(lines)-1 {
109: 			if count > 1 {
110: 				result = append(result, line+" (x"+strconv.Itoa(count)+")")
111: 			} else {
112: 				result = append(result, line)
113: 			}
114: 		}
115: 	}
116: 
117: 	return strings.Join(result, "\n")
118: }
119: 
120: // stripTimestamp removes timestamp prefix from a line.
121: func (f *LogAggregator) stripTimestamp(line string) string {
122: 	for _, pattern := range f.timestampPatterns {
123: 		line = pattern.ReplaceAllString(line, "")
124: 	}
125: 	return strings.TrimSpace(line)
126: }

128: // aggregateTestResults compresses test output into summaries.
129: // Aggregates multiple test suites into single line.
130: func (f *LogAggregator) aggregateTestResults(input string) string {
131: 	// Check for test output patterns
132: 	hasTestOutput := false
133: 	for _, pattern := range TestResultPatterns {
134: 		if pattern.MatchString(input) {
135: 			hasTestOutput = true
136: 			break
137: 		}
138: 	}
139: 
140: 	if !hasTestOutput {
141: 		return input
142: 	}
143: 
144: 	lines := strings.Split(input, "\n")
145: 	var result []string
146: 	var testSuites []testSuite
147: 	currentSuite := testSuite{}
148: 	inTestOutput := false
149: 
150: 	for _, line := range lines {
151: 		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
152: 
153: 		// Detect test suite boundaries
154: 		if strings.Contains(trimmed, "test result:") ||
155: 			strings.Contains(trimmed, "PASS") ||
156: 			strings.Contains(trimmed, "FAIL") {
157: 			inTestOutput = true
158: 
159: 			// Parse test results
160: 			if strings.Contains(trimmed, "passed") {
161: 				currentSuite.Passed = f.extractNumber(trimmed, "passed")
162: 			}
163: 			if strings.Contains(trimmed, "failed") {
164: 				currentSuite.Failed = f.extractNumber(trimmed, "failed")
165: 			}
166: 			if strings.Contains(trimmed, "ignored") || strings.Contains(trimmed, "skipped") {
167: 				currentSuite.Ignored = f.extractNumber(trimmed, "ignored")
168: 				currentSuite.Ignored += f.extractNumber(trimmed, "skipped")
169: 			}
170: 
171: 			// Check for failure
172: 			if strings.Contains(trimmed, "FAILED") || strings.Contains(trimmed, "FAIL") {
173: 				currentSuite.Failed++
174: 			}
175: 
176: 			// Finalize suite
177: 			if strings.Contains(trimmed, "test result:") ||
178: 				(strings.Contains(trimmed, "PASS") && !strings.Contains(trimmed, "passed")) {
179: 				testSuites = append(testSuites, currentSuite)
180: 				currentSuite = testSuite{}
181: 			}
182: 		} else if !inTestOutput {
183: 			result = append(result, line)
184: 		}
185: 	}
186: 
187: 	// Generate summary
188: 	if len(testSuites) > 0 {
189: 		totalPassed := 0
190: 		totalFailed := 0
191: 		totalIgnored := 0
192: 
193: 		for _, suite := range testSuites {
194: 			totalPassed += suite.Passed
195: 			totalFailed += suite.Failed
196: 			totalIgnored += suite.Ignored
197: 		}
198: 
199: 		// If no failures, show compact summary
200: 		if totalFailed == 0 && totalPassed > 0 {
201: 			summary := "✓ " + strconv.Itoa(totalPassed) + " passed"
202: 			if totalIgnored > 0 {
203: 				summary += ", " + strconv.Itoa(totalIgnored) + " ignored"
204: 			}
205: 			summary += " (" + strconv.Itoa(len(testSuites)) + " suites)"
206: 			result = append(result, "", summary)
207: 		} else {
208: 			// Show failures in detail
209: 			summary := "✗ " + strconv.Itoa(totalFailed) + " failed, " +
210: 				strconv.Itoa(totalPassed) + " passed"
211: 			result = append(result, "", summary)
212: 		}
213: 	}
214: 
215: 	return strings.Join(result, "\n")
216: }
217: 
218: type testSuite struct {
219: 	Passed  int
220: 	Failed  int
221: 	Ignored int
222: }
223: 
224: // aggregateBuildOutput compresses build/compilation output.
225: func (f *LogAggregator) aggregateBuildOutput(input string) string {
226: 	lines := strings.Split(input, "\n")
227: 	var result []string
228: 	warningCount := 0
229: 	errorCount := 0
230: 
231: 	for _, line := range lines {
232: 		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
233: 		lower := strings.ToLower(trimmed)
234: 
235: 		// Count warnings and errors
236: 		if strings.Contains(lower, "warning:") {
237: 			warningCount++
238: 			// Keep first few warnings
239: 			if warningCount <= 3 {
240: 				result = append(result, line)
241: 			}
242: 			continue
243: 		}
244: 
245: 		if strings.Contains(lower, "error:") {
246: 			errorCount++
247: 			result = append(result, line)
248: 			continue
249: 		}
250: 
251: 		result = append(result, line)
252: 	}
253: 
254: 	// Add summary if we condensed warnings
255: 	if warningCount > 3 {
256: 		result = append(result, "",
257: 			"... and "+strconv.Itoa(warningCount-3)+" more warnings")
258: 	}
259: 
260: 	return strings.Join(result, "\n")
261: }
262: 
263: // extractNumber extracts a number preceding a keyword.
264: func (f *LogAggregator) extractNumber(text string, keyword string) int {
265: 	// Pattern: "N keyword"
266: 	re := regexp.MustCompile(`(\d+)\s+` + keyword)
267: 	matches := re.FindStringSubmatch(text)
268: 	if len(matches) > 1 {
269: 		n, _ := strconv.Atoi(matches[1])
270: 		return n
271: 	}
272: 	return 0
273: }

277: 	filter := NewLogAggregator()
278: 	output, _ := filter.Apply(input, ModeMinimal)

282: // GroupLines groups lines by common prefix or pattern.
283: func GroupLines(input string) string {
284: 	lines := strings.Split(input, "\n")
285: 
286: 	// Group by first word/pattern
287: 	groups := make(map[string][]string)
288: 	var order []string
289: 
290: 	for _, line := range lines {
291: 		if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
292: 			continue
293: 		}
294: 
295: 		// Extract key (first word or pattern)
296: 		fields := strings.Fields(line)
297: 		if len(fields) == 0 {
298: 			continue
299: 		}
300: 
301: 		key := fields[0]
302: 		if len(key) > 20 {
303: 			key = key[:20]
304: 		}
305: 
306: 		if _, exists := groups[key]; !exists {
307: 			order = append(order, key)
308: 		}
309: 		groups[key] = append(groups[key], line)
310: 	}
311: 
312: 	// Reconstruct with groups
313: 	var result []string
314: 	for _, key := range order {
315: 		lines := groups[key]
316: 		if len(lines) > 1 {
317: 			result = append(result, "["+key+"] ("+strconv.Itoa(len(lines))+" lines)")
318: 		} else {
319: 			result = append(result, lines[0])
320: 		}
321: 	}
322: 
323: 	return strings.Join(result, "\n")
324: }

83: 	}
84: 
85: 	if !t.config.Enabled {
86: 		return false
87: 	}
88: 
89: 	switch t.config.Mode {
90: 	case ModeNever:
91: 		return false
92: 	case ModeFailures:
93: 		if exitCode == 0 {
94: 			return false
95: 		}
96: 	case ModeAlways:
97: 		// Continue
98: 	}
99: 
100: 	// Skip small outputs
101: 	if rawLen < MinTeeSize {
102: 		return false
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	return true
106: }
107: 
108: // Write writes the raw output to a tee file.
109: // Returns the file path if written, or empty string if skipped.
110: func (t *Tee) Write(raw string, commandSlug string, exitCode int) string {
111: 	if !t.ShouldTee(len(raw), exitCode) {
112: 		return ""
113: 	}
114: 
115: 	dir, err := t.GetDirectory()
116: 	if err != nil {
117: 		return ""
118: 	}
119: 
120: 	// Create directory if needed
121: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
122: 		return ""
123: 	}
124: 
125: 	// Sanitize slug for filename
126: 	slug := sanitizeSlug(commandSlug)
127: 
128: 	// Generate filename with epoch timestamp
129: 	epoch := time.Now().Unix()
130: 	filename := fmt.Sprintf("%d_%s.log", epoch, slug)
131: 	filepath := filepath.Join(dir, filename)
132: 
133: 	// Truncate if needed
134: 	content := raw
135: 	if len(raw) > t.config.MaxFileSize {
136: 		content = fmt.Sprintf("%s\n\n--- truncated at %d bytes ---",
137: 			raw[:t.config.MaxFileSize], t.config.MaxFileSize)
138: 	}
139: 
140: 	// Write file
141: 	if err := os.WriteFile(filepath, []byte(content), 0644); err != nil {
142: 		return ""
143: 	}
144: 
145: 	// Rotate old files
146: 	t.cleanupOldFiles(dir)
147: 
148: 	return filepath
149: }
150: 
151: // WriteAndHint writes the raw output and returns a formatted hint.
152: func (t *Tee) WriteAndHint(raw string, commandSlug string, exitCode int) string {
153: 	path := t.Write(raw, commandSlug, exitCode)
154: 	if path == "" {
155: 		return ""
156: 	}
157: 	return FormatHint(path)
158: }
159: 
160: // cleanupOldFiles removes old tee files, keeping only the newest MaxFiles.
161: func (t *Tee) cleanupOldFiles(dir string) {
162: 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
163: 	if err != nil {
164: 		return
165: 	}
166: 
167: 	// Filter .log files
168: 	var logFiles []os.DirEntry
169: 	for _, entry := range entries {
170: 		if !entry.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".log") {
171: 			logFiles = append(logFiles, entry)
172: 		}
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	if len(logFiles) <= t.config.MaxFiles {
176: 		return
177: 	}
178: 
179: 	// Sort by name (starts with epoch timestamp, so chronological)
180: 	// We want to keep newest, so sort ascending and remove oldest
181: 	sorted := make([]string, len(logFiles))
182: 	for i, e := range logFiles {
183: 		sorted[i] = e.Name()
184: 	}
185: 
186: 	// Simple sort (filenames start with epoch)
187: 	for i := 0; i < len(sorted)-1; i++ {
188: 		for j := i + 1; j < len(sorted); j++ {
189: 			if sorted[i] > sorted[j] {
190: 				sorted[i], sorted[j] = sorted[j], sorted[i]
191: 			}
192: 		}
193: 	}
194: 
195: 	// Remove oldest files
196: 	toRemove := len(sorted) - t.config.MaxFiles
197: 	for i := 0; i < toRemove; i++ {
198: 		os.Remove(filepath.Join(dir, sorted[i]))
199: 	}
200: }
201: 
202: // sanitizeSlug sanitizes a command slug for use in filenames.
203: // Replaces non-alphanumeric chars (except underscore/hyphen) with underscore,
204: // truncates at 40 chars.
205: func sanitizeSlug(slug string) string {
206: 	// Replace non-alphanumeric (except _ and -) with _
207: 	re := regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]`)
208: 	sanitized := re.ReplaceAllString(slug, "_")
209: 
210: 	// Truncate at 40 chars
211: 	if len(sanitized) > 40 {
212: 		sanitized = sanitized[:40]
213: 	}
214: 
215: 	return sanitized
216: }
217: 
218: // FormatHint formats a file path as a hint string with ~ shorthand.
219: func FormatHint(path string) string {
220: 	home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()
221: 	display := path
222: 
223: 	if home != "" {
224: 		if strings.HasPrefix(path, home) {
225: 			display = "~" + strings.TrimPrefix(path, home)
226: 		}
227: 	}
228: 
229: 	return fmt.Sprintf("[full output: %s]", display)
230: }
231: 
232: // Global default tee instance
233: var defaultTee = New(DefaultConfig())
234: 
235: // Write writes using the default tee instance.
236: func Write(raw string, commandSlug string, exitCode int) string {
237: 	return defaultTee.Write(raw, commandSlug, exitCode)
238: }
239: 
240: // WriteAndHint writes and returns hint using the default tee instance.
241: func WriteAndHint(raw string, commandSlug string, exitCode int) string {
242: 	return defaultTee.WriteAndHint(raw, commandSlug, exitCode)
243: }

13: // Metrics represents usage metrics from ccusage for a single period

17: 	CacheCreationTokens uint64  `json:"cacheCreationTokens"`

21: }

23: // Period represents period data with key (date/month/week) and metrics
24: type Period struct {

26: 	Metrics Metrics
27: }
28: 
29: // Granularity represents time granularity for ccusage reports
30: type Granularity int

34: 	Weekly
35: 	Monthly
36: )
37:

39: type dailyResponse struct {

41: }
42:

63: 	Metrics
64: }

66: // binaryExists checks if ccusage binary exists in PATH
67: func binaryExists() bool {
68: 	_, err := exec.LookPath("ccusage")
69: 	return err == nil
70: }
71: 
72: // buildCommand builds the ccusage command, falling back to npx if binary not in PATH
73: func buildCommand() (*exec.Cmd, bool) {
74: 	if binaryExists() {
75: 		return exec.Command("ccusage"), true
76: 	}
77: 
78: 	// Fallback: try npx
79: 	npxCheck := exec.Command("npx", "ccusage", "--help")
80: 	npxCheck.Stdout = nil
81: 	npxCheck.Stderr = nil
82: 	if err := npxCheck.Run(); err == nil {
83: 		return exec.Command("npx", "ccusage"), true
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	return nil, false
87: }
88: 
89: // IsAvailable checks if ccusage CLI is available (binary or via npx)
90: func IsAvailable() bool {

96: //
97: // Returns nil if ccusage is unavailable (graceful degradation).
98: // Returns parsed data on success.
99: // Returns error only on unexpected failures (JSON parse, etc.).
100: func Fetch(granularity Granularity) ([]Period, error) {
101: 	cmd, ok := buildCommand()
102: 	if !ok {
103: 		fmt.Fprintln(stderr, "⚠️  ccusage not found. Install: npm i -g ccusage (or use npx ccusage)")
104: 		return nil, nil
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	subcommand := "daily"
108: 	switch granularity {
109: 	case Weekly:
110: 		subcommand = "weekly"
111: 	case Monthly:
112: 		subcommand = "monthly"
113: 	}
114: 
115: 	cmd.Args = append(cmd.Args, subcommand, "--json", "--since", "20250101")
116: 	output, err := cmd.Output()
117: 	if err != nil {
118: 		if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
119: 			fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "⚠️  ccusage exited with %s\n", exitErr.Error())
120: 		} else {

122: 		}

129: // stderrWriter is used for writing warnings to stderr

141: 	switch granularity {

146: 		}
147: 		periods := make([]Period, len(resp.Daily))
148: 		for i, e := range resp.Daily {
149: 			periods[i] = Period{Key: e.Date, Metrics: e.Metrics}
150: 		}
151: 		return periods, nil
152: 
153: 	case Weekly:
154: 		var resp weeklyResponse
155: 		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(jsonStr), &resp); err != nil {
156: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid JSON structure for weekly data: %w", err)
157: 		}
158: 		periods := make([]Period, len(resp.Weekly))
159: 		for i, e := range resp.Weekly {
160: 			periods[i] = Period{Key: e.Week, Metrics: e.Metrics}
161: 		}
162: 		return periods, nil
163: 
164: 	case Monthly:
165: 		var resp monthlyResponse
166: 		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(jsonStr), &resp); err != nil {
167: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid JSON structure for monthly data: %w", err)
168: 		}
169: 		periods := make([]Period, len(resp.Monthly))
170: 		for i, e := range resp.Monthly {
171: 			periods[i] = Period{Key: e.Month, Metrics: e.Metrics}
172: 		}
173: 		return periods, nil
174: 	}
175: 
176: 	return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown granularity: %d", granularity)
177: }

2: 
3: import (
4: 	"crypto/sha256"

14: 
15: // Configuration
16: const (
17: 	PingIntervalSecs = 23 * 3600 // 23 hours between pings
18: )
19: 
20: // TelemetryURL and Token can be set at build time via ldflags.
21: var (
22: 	TelemetryURL   string
23: 	TelemetryToken string
24: )
25: 
26: // StatsProvider defines the interface for getting tracking stats.
27: type StatsProvider interface {
28: 	CountCommandsSince(since time.Time) (int64, error)
29: 	TopCommands(limit int) ([]string, error)
30: 	OverallSavingsPct() (float64, error)
31: 	TokensSaved24h() (int64, error)
32: 	TokensSavedTotal() (int64, error)
33: }
34: 
35: // Client handles telemetry ping operations.
36: type Client struct {
37: 	url     string
38: 	token   string
39: 	dataDir string
40: 	enabled bool
41: 	stats   StatsProvider
42: }
43: 
44: // NewClient creates a new telemetry client.
45: func NewClient(stats StatsProvider) *Client {
46: 	home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()
47: 	dataDir := filepath.Join(home, ".local", "share", "tokman")
48: 
49: 	return &Client{
50: 		url:     TelemetryURL,
51: 		token:   TelemetryToken,
52: 		dataDir: dataDir,
53: 		enabled: TelemetryURL != "",
54: 		stats:   stats,
55: 	}
56: }
57: 
58: // MaybePing sends a telemetry ping if enabled and not already sent today.
59: // This is fire-and-forget: errors are silently ignored.
60: func (c *Client) MaybePing() {
61: 	// No URL configured → telemetry disabled
62: 	if !c.enabled || c.url == "" {
63: 		return
64: 	}
65: 
66: 	// Check opt-out: environment variable
67: 	if os.Getenv("TOKMAN_TELEMETRY_DISABLED") == "1" {
68: 		return
69: 	}
70: 
71: 	// Check opt-out: config file (handled by caller)
72: 
73: 	// Check last ping time
74: 	marker := c.markerPath()
75: 	if info, err := os.Stat(marker); err == nil {
76: 		if time.Since(info.ModTime()).Seconds() < PingIntervalSecs {
77: 			return // Already pinged recently
78: 		}
79: 	}
80: 
81: 	// Touch marker immediately (before sending) to avoid double-ping
82: 	c.touchMarker(marker)
83: 
84: 	// Send ping in background (never block the CLI)
85: 	go c.sendPing()
86: }
87: 
88: func (c *Client) sendPing() {
89: 	deviceHash := generateDeviceHash()
90: 	version := getVersion()
91: 	osName := runtime.GOOS
92: 	arch := runtime.GOARCH
93: 	installMethod := detectInstallMethod()
94: 
95: 	// Get stats from tracking DB
96: 	var commands24h int64
97: 	var topCommands []string
98: 	var savingsPct float64
99: 	var tokensSaved24h, tokensSavedTotal int64
100: 
101: 	if c.stats != nil {
102: 		commands24h, _ = c.stats.CountCommandsSince(time.Now().Add(-24 * time.Hour))
103: 		topCommands, _ = c.stats.TopCommands(5)
104: 		savingsPct, _ = c.stats.OverallSavingsPct()
105: 		tokensSaved24h, _ = c.stats.TokensSaved24h()
106: 		tokensSavedTotal, _ = c.stats.TokensSavedTotal()
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	// Build payload (JSON-like structure)
110: 	payload := fmt.Sprintf(`{"device_hash":"%s","version":"%s","os":"%s","arch":"%s","install_method":"%s","commands_24h":%d,"top_commands":%s,"savings_pct":%.2f,"tokens_saved_24h":%d,"tokens_saved_total":%d}`,
111: 		deviceHash, version, osName, arch, installMethod, commands24h, formatStringSlice(topCommands), savingsPct, tokensSaved24h, tokensSavedTotal)
112: 
113: 	// Send HTTP POST (with 2-second timeout)
114: 	// Using curl for simplicity (no external HTTP dependencies)
115: 	cmd := exec.Command("curl", "-s", "-X", "POST",
116: 		"-H", "Content-Type: application/json",
117: 		"-H", fmt.Sprintf("X-TokMan-Token: %s", c.token),
118: 		"-m", "2", // 2-second timeout
119: 		"-d", payload,
120: 		c.url,
121: 	)
122: 	cmd.Run() // Ignore errors (fire-and-forget)
123: }
124: 
125: func (c *Client) markerPath() string {
126: 	return filepath.Join(c.dataDir, ".telemetry_last_ping")
127: }
128: 
129: func (c *Client) touchMarker(path string) {
130: 	os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0755)
131: 	os.WriteFile(path, []byte{}, 0644)
132: }
133: 
134: // generateDeviceHash creates an anonymous SHA-256 hash of hostname:username.
135: func generateDeviceHash() string {
136: 	hostname, _ := os.Hostname()
137: 	username := os.Getenv("USER")
138: 	if username == "" {
139: 		username = os.Getenv("USERNAME")
140: 	}
141: 
142: 	data := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", hostname, username)
143: 	hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(data))
144: 	return hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])
145: }

147: func getVersion() string {
148: 	// This could be set via ldflags at build time
149: 	if v := os.Getenv("TOKMAN_VERSION"); v != "" {
150: 		return v
151: 	}
152: 	return "dev"
153: }
154: 
155: func formatStringSlice(s []string) string {
156: 	if len(s) == 0 {
157: 		return "[]"
158: 	}
159: 	quoted := make([]string, len(s))
160: 	for i, v := range s {
161: 		quoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(`"%s"`, v)
162: 	}
163: 	return fmt.Sprintf("[%s]", strings.Join(quoted, ","))
164: }

166: // detectInstallMethod determines how tokman was installed.
167: func detectInstallMethod() string {
168: 	// Check if running from GOPATH/bin (go install)
169: 	execPath, err := os.Executable()
170: 	if err == nil {
171: 		// Check common install locations
172: 		home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()
173: 		goBinPath := filepath.Join(home, "go", "bin")
174: 		if strings.HasPrefix(execPath, goBinPath) {
175: 			return "go-install"
176: 		}
177: 		// Check for Homebrew on macOS
178: 		if strings.HasPrefix(execPath, "/usr/local/Cellar/") || strings.HasPrefix(execPath, "/opt/homebrew/Cellar/") {
179: 			return "homebrew"
180: 		}
181: 		// Check for Linux package manager paths
182: 		if strings.HasPrefix(execPath, "/usr/bin/") || strings.HasPrefix(execPath, "/usr/local/bin/") {
183: 			return "package-manager"
184: 		}
185: 	}
186: 	return "unknown"
187: }
188: 
189: // Global client
190: var defaultClient *Client
191: 
192: // Init initializes the global telemetry client.
193: func Init(stats StatsProvider) {
194: 	defaultClient = NewClient(stats)
195: }
196: 
197: // MaybePing sends a telemetry ping using the global client.
198: func MaybePing() {
199: 	if defaultClient != nil {
200: 		defaultClient.MaybePing()
201: 	}
202: }

205: func IsEnabled() bool {
206: 	return TelemetryURL != ""
207: }
208: 
209: // IsOptedOut checks if telemetry is explicitly disabled.
210: func IsOptedOut() bool {
211: 	return os.Getenv("TOKMAN_TELEMETRY_DISABLED") == "1"
212: }

43: // Track records the execution with token savings.

45: 	t.once.Do(func() {
46: 		execTime := time.Since(t.startTime)

64: 			ProjectPath:    cwd,

116: 	}

120: // NewTracker creates a new Tracker with the given database path.
121: func NewTracker(dbPath string) (*Tracker, error) {
122: 	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dbPath)
123: 	if err != nil {
124: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open database: %w", err)
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	// Enable WAL mode for better performance
128: 	if _, err := db.Exec("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL"); err != nil {
129: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to enable WAL mode: %w", err)
130: 	}
131: 
132: 	// Run migrations
133: 	for _, migration := range InitSchema() {
134: 		if _, err := db.Exec(migration); err != nil {
135: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to run migration: %w", err)
136: 		}
137: 	}
138: 
139: 	return &Tracker{db: db}, nil
140: }

142: // Close closes the database connection.
143: func (t *Tracker) Close() error {
144: 	return t.db.Close()
145: }

147: // Query executes a raw SQL query and returns the rows.
148: // This is exposed for custom aggregations in the economics package.
149: func (t *Tracker) Query(query string, args ...interface{}) (*sql.Rows, error) {
150: 	return t.db.Query(query, args...)
151: }

153: // EstimateTokens provides a heuristic token count.
154: // Uses the formula: ceil(text.length / 4.0)
155: func EstimateTokens(text string) int {
156: 	return (len(text) + 3) / 4
157: }

159: // Record saves a command execution to the database.
160: func (t *Tracker) Record(record *CommandRecord) error {
161: 	query := `
162: 		INSERT INTO commands (
163: 			command, original_output, filtered_output,
164: 			original_tokens, filtered_tokens, saved_tokens,
165: 			project_path, session_id, exec_time_ms, parse_success
166: 		) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
167: 	`
168: 
169: 	result, err := t.db.Exec(query,
170: 		record.Command,
171: 		record.OriginalOutput,
172: 		record.FilteredOutput,
173: 		record.OriginalTokens,
174: 		record.FilteredTokens,
175: 		record.SavedTokens,
176: 		record.ProjectPath,
177: 		record.SessionID,
178: 		record.ExecTimeMs,
179: 		record.ParseSuccess,
180: 	)
181: 	if err != nil {
182: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to record command: %w", err)
183: 	}
184: 
185: 	id, err := result.LastInsertId()
186: 	if err == nil {
187: 		record.ID = id
188: 	}
189: 
190: 	// Run cleanup after recording (fire-and-forget, don't block)
191: 	go t.cleanupOld()
192: 
193: 	return nil
194: }

196: // cleanupOld removes records older than HistoryRetentionDays.
197: // This is called automatically after each Record operation.
198: func (t *Tracker) cleanupOld() {
199: 	cutoff := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -HistoryRetentionDays)
200: 	_, _ = t.db.Exec(
201: 		"DELETE FROM commands WHERE timestamp < ?",
202: 		cutoff.Format(time.RFC3339),
203: 	)
204: }

206: // CleanupOld manually triggers cleanup of old records.
207: // Returns the number of records deleted.
208: func (t *Tracker) CleanupOld() (int64, error) {
209: 	cutoff := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -HistoryRetentionDays)
210: 	result, err := t.db.Exec(
211: 		"DELETE FROM commands WHERE timestamp < ?",
212: 		cutoff.Format(time.RFC3339),
213: 	)
214: 	if err != nil {
215: 		return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to cleanup old records: %w", err)
216: 	}
217: 	return result.RowsAffected()
218: }

222: func (t *Tracker) GetSavings(projectPath string) (*SavingsSummary, error) {
223: 	query := `
224: 		SELECT 
225: 			COUNT(*) as total_commands,
226: 			COALESCE(SUM(saved_tokens), 0) as total_saved,
227: 			COALESCE(SUM(original_tokens), 0) as total_original,
228: 			COALESCE(SUM(filtered_tokens), 0) as total_filtered
229: 		FROM commands

231: 	`

233: 	pattern := projectPath + "/*"

237: 		&summary.TotalCommands,

239: 		&summary.TotalOriginal,
240: 		&summary.TotalFiltered,
241: 	)
242: 	if err != nil {

244: 	}

246: 	if summary.TotalOriginal > 0 {

323: func (t *Tracker) GetCommandStats(projectPath string) ([]CommandStats, error) {
324: 	query := `
325: 		SELECT 
326: 			command,
327: 			COUNT(*) as execution_count,
328: 			COALESCE(SUM(saved_tokens), 0) as total_saved,
329: 			COALESCE(SUM(original_tokens), 0) as total_original
330: 		FROM commands

333: 		ORDER BY total_saved DESC

363: 		FROM commands

365: 		ORDER BY timestamp DESC

393: // GetDailySavings returns token savings grouped by day.
394: func (t *Tracker) GetDailySavings(projectPath string, days int) ([]struct {
395: 	Date     string
396: 	Saved    int
397: 	Original int
398: 	Commands int

401: 		SELECT

408: 		  AND timestamp >= DATE('now', ?)
409: 		GROUP BY DATE(timestamp)

443: // RecordFallback records a command that wasn't recognized (parse failure).
444: func (t *Tracker) RecordFallback(command string, projectPath string, output string, execTimeMs int64) error {
445: 	originalTokens := EstimateTokens(output)
446: 	return t.Record(&CommandRecord{
447: 		Command:        command,
448: 		OriginalOutput: output,
449: 		FilteredOutput: output,
450: 		OriginalTokens: originalTokens,
451: 		FilteredTokens: originalTokens,
452: 		SavedTokens:    0,
453: 		ProjectPath:    projectPath,
454: 		ExecTimeMs:     execTimeMs,
455: 		Timestamp:      time.Now(),
456: 		ParseSuccess:   false,
457: 	})

460: // ParseFailureRecord represents a single parse failure event.
461: type ParseFailureRecord struct {
462: 	ID                int64     `json:"id"`
463: 	Timestamp         time.Time `json:"timestamp"`
464: 	RawCommand        string    `json:"raw_command"`
465: 	ErrorMessage      string    `json:"error_message"`
466: 	FallbackSucceeded bool      `json:"fallback_succeeded"`
467: }

469: // ParseFailureSummary represents aggregated parse failure analytics.
470: type ParseFailureSummary struct {
471: 	Total          int64                 `json:"total"`
472: 	RecoveryRate   float64               `json:"recovery_rate"`
473: 	TopCommands    []CommandFailureCount `json:"top_commands"`
474: 	RecentFailures []ParseFailureRecord  `json:"recent_failures"`
475: }

478: type CommandFailureCount struct {

483: // RecordParseFailure records a parse failure event.
484: func (t *Tracker) RecordParseFailure(rawCommand string, errorMessage string, fallbackSucceeded bool) error {
485: 	_, err := t.db.Exec(
486: 		`INSERT INTO parse_failures (timestamp, raw_command, error_message, fallback_succeeded)
487: 		 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
488: 		time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339),
489: 		rawCommand,
490: 		errorMessage,
491: 		fallbackSucceeded,
492: 	)
493: 	if err != nil {
494: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to record parse failure: %w", err)
495: 	}
496: 
497: 	// Cleanup old records
498: 	go func() {
499: 		cutoff := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -HistoryRetentionDays)
500: 		t.db.Exec("DELETE FROM parse_failures WHERE timestamp < ?", cutoff.Format(time.RFC3339))
501: 	}()
502: 
503: 	return nil
504: }
505: 
506: // GetParseFailureSummary returns aggregated parse failure analytics.
507: func (t *Tracker) GetParseFailureSummary() (*ParseFailureSummary, error) {
508: 	summary := &ParseFailureSummary{}
509: 
510: 	// Get total count
511: 	err := t.db.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM parse_failures").Scan(&summary.Total)
512: 	if err != nil {
513: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get parse failure count: %w", err)
514: 	}
515: 
516: 	if summary.Total == 0 {
517: 		return summary, nil
518: 	}
519: 
520: 	// Get recovery rate
521: 	var succeeded int64
522: 	err = t.db.QueryRow(
523: 		"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM parse_failures WHERE fallback_succeeded = 1",
524: 	).Scan(&succeeded)
525: 	if err == nil {
526: 		summary.RecoveryRate = float64(succeeded) / float64(summary.Total) * 100
527: 	}
528: 
529: 	// Get top 10 failing commands
530: 	rows, err := t.db.Query(
531: 		`SELECT raw_command, COUNT(*) as cnt
532: 		 FROM parse_failures
533: 		 GROUP BY raw_command
534: 		 ORDER BY cnt DESC
535: 		 LIMIT 10`,
536: 	)
537: 	if err == nil {
538: 		defer rows.Close()
539: 		for rows.Next() {
540: 			var cfc CommandFailureCount
541: 			if err := rows.Scan(&cfc.Command, &cfc.Count); err == nil {
542: 				summary.TopCommands = append(summary.TopCommands, cfc)
543: 			}
544: 		}
545: 	}
546: 
547: 	// Get recent 10 failures
548: 	rows, err = t.db.Query(
549: 		`SELECT id, timestamp, raw_command, error_message, fallback_succeeded
550: 		 FROM parse_failures
551: 		 ORDER BY timestamp DESC
552: 		 LIMIT 10`,
553: 	)
554: 	if err == nil {
555: 		defer rows.Close()
556: 		for rows.Next() {
557: 			var pfr ParseFailureRecord
558: 			var ts string
559: 			var fb int
560: 			if err := rows.Scan(&pfr.ID, &ts, &pfr.RawCommand, &pfr.ErrorMessage, &fb); err == nil {
561: 				pfr.Timestamp, _ = time.Parse(time.RFC3339, ts)
562: 				pfr.FallbackSucceeded = fb == 1
563: 				summary.RecentFailures = append(summary.RecentFailures, pfr)
564: 			}
565: 		}
566: 	}
567: 
568: 	return summary, nil
569: }

174: 	// Insert ccusage data
175: 	for _, entry := range cc {
176: 		if _, exists := periodMap[entry.Key]; !exists {
177: 			periodMap[entry.Key] = &PeriodEconomics{Label: entry.Key}
178: 		}
179: 		p := periodMap[entry.Key]
180: 		p.CCCost = &entry.Metrics.TotalCost
181: 		p.CCTotalTokens = &entry.Metrics.TotalTokens
182: 		p.CCInputTokens = &entry.Metrics.InputTokens
183: 		p.CCOutputTokens = &entry.Metrics.OutputTokens
184: 		p.CCCacheCreateTokens = &entry.Metrics.CacheCreationTokens
185: 		p.CCCacheReadTokens = &entry.Metrics.CacheReadTokens
186: 		active := entry.Metrics.InputTokens + entry.Metrics.OutputTokens
187: 		p.CCActiveTokens = &active
188: 	}
189: 
190: 	// Merge tokman data
191: 	for _, entry := range tm {
192: 		if _, exists := periodMap[entry.Date]; !exists {
193: 			periodMap[entry.Date] = &PeriodEconomics{Label: entry.Date}
194: 		}
195: 		p := periodMap[entry.Date]
196: 		p.TMCommands = &entry.Commands
197: 		p.TMSavedTokens = &entry.SavedTokens
198: 		p.TMSavingsPct = &entry.SavingsPct
199: 	}
200: 
201: 	// Compute metrics and sort
202: 	result := make([]PeriodEconomics, 0, len(periodMap))
203: 	for _, p := range periodMap {
204: 		p.computeWeightedMetrics()
205: 		p.computeDualMetrics()
206: 		result = append(result, *p)
207: 	}
208: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
209: 		return result[i].Label < result[j].Label
210: 	})
211: 
212: 	return result
213: }

215: func (p *PeriodEconomics) computeWeightedMetrics() {
216: 	if p.CCCost == nil || p.TMSavedTokens == nil {
217: 		return
218: 	}
219: 	if p.CCInputTokens == nil || p.CCOutputTokens == nil ||
220: 		p.CCCacheCreateTokens == nil || p.CCCacheReadTokens == nil {
221: 		return
222: 	}
223: 
224: 	// Weighted units = input + 5*output + 1.25*cache_create + 0.1*cache_read
225: 	weightedUnits := float64(*p.CCInputTokens) +
226: 		WeightOutput*float64(*p.CCOutputTokens) +
227: 		WeightCacheCreate*float64(*p.CCCacheCreateTokens) +
228: 		WeightCacheRead*float64(*p.CCCacheReadTokens)
229: 
230: 	if weightedUnits <= 0 {
231: 		return
232: 	}
233: 
234: 	inputCPT := *p.CCCost / weightedUnits
235: 	savings := float64(*p.TMSavedTokens) * inputCPT
236: 
237: 	p.WeightedInputCPT = &inputCPT
238: 	p.SavingsWeighted = &savings
239: }
240: 
241: func (p *PeriodEconomics) computeDualMetrics() {
242: 	if p.CCCost == nil || p.TMSavedTokens == nil {
243: 		return
244: 	}
245: 
246: 	// Blended CPT (cost / total_tokens including cache)
247: 	if p.CCTotalTokens != nil && *p.CCTotalTokens > 0 {
248: 		blended := *p.CCCost / float64(*p.CCTotalTokens)
249: 		p.BlendedCPT = &blended
250: 		savings := float64(*p.TMSavedTokens) * blended
251: 		p.SavingsBlended = &savings
252: 	}
253: 
254: 	// Active CPT (cost / active_tokens = input+output only)
255: 	if p.CCActiveTokens != nil && *p.CCActiveTokens > 0 {
256: 		active := *p.CCCost / float64(*p.CCActiveTokens)
257: 		p.ActiveCPT = &active
258: 		savings := float64(*p.TMSavedTokens) * active
259: 		p.SavingsActive = &savings
260: 	}
261: }

263: func computeTotals(periods []PeriodEconomics) Totals {
264: 	var totals Totals
265: 	var pctSum float64
266: 	var pctCount int
267: 
268: 	for _, p := range periods {
269: 		if p.CCCost != nil {
270: 			totals.CCCost += *p.CCCost
271: 		}
272: 		if p.CCTotalTokens != nil {
273: 			totals.CCTotalTokens += *p.CCTotalTokens
274: 		}
275: 		if p.CCActiveTokens != nil {
276: 			totals.CCActiveTokens += *p.CCActiveTokens
277: 		}
278: 		if p.CCInputTokens != nil {
279: 			totals.CCInputTokens += *p.CCInputTokens
280: 		}
281: 		if p.CCOutputTokens != nil {
282: 			totals.CCOutputTokens += *p.CCOutputTokens
283: 		}
284: 		if p.CCCacheCreateTokens != nil {
285: 			totals.CCCacheCreate += *p.CCCacheCreateTokens
286: 		}
287: 		if p.CCCacheReadTokens != nil {
288: 			totals.CCCacheRead += *p.CCCacheReadTokens
289: 		}
290: 		if p.TMCommands != nil {
291: 			totals.TMCommands += *p.TMCommands
292: 		}
293: 		if p.TMSavedTokens != nil {
294: 			totals.TMSavedTokens += *p.TMSavedTokens
295: 		}
296: 		if p.TMSavingsPct != nil {
297: 			pctSum += *p.TMSavingsPct
298: 			pctCount++
299: 		}
300: 	}
301: 
302: 	if pctCount > 0 {
303: 		totals.TMAvgSavingsPct = pctSum / float64(pctCount)
304: 	}
305: 
306: 	// Compute global weighted metrics
307: 	weightedUnits := float64(totals.CCInputTokens) +
308: 		WeightOutput*float64(totals.CCOutputTokens) +
309: 		WeightCacheCreate*float64(totals.CCCacheCreate) +
310: 		WeightCacheRead*float64(totals.CCCacheRead)
311: 
312: 	if weightedUnits > 0 {
313: 		inputCPT := totals.CCCost / weightedUnits
314: 		totals.WeightedInputCPT = &inputCPT
315: 		savings := float64(totals.TMSavedTokens) * inputCPT
316: 		totals.SavingsWeighted = &savings
317: 	}
318: 
319: 	// Compute global dual metrics
320: 	if totals.CCTotalTokens > 0 {
321: 		blended := totals.CCCost / float64(totals.CCTotalTokens)
322: 		totals.BlendedCPT = &blended
323: 		savings := float64(totals.TMSavedTokens) * blended
324: 		totals.SavingsBlended = &savings
325: 	}
326: 	if totals.CCActiveTokens > 0 {
327: 		active := totals.CCCost / float64(totals.CCActiveTokens)
328: 		totals.ActiveCPT = &active
329: 		savings := float64(totals.TMSavedTokens) * active
330: 		totals.SavingsActive = &savings
331: 	}
332: 
333: 	return totals
334: }

511: 		p.CCCacheReadTokens = &entry.Metrics.CacheReadTokens

529: 	// Compute metrics and sort

531: 	for _, p := range periodMap {
532: 		p.computeWeightedMetrics()
533: 		p.computeDualMetrics()
534: 		result = append(result, *p)
535: 	}
536: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
537: 		return result[i].Label < result[j].Label
538: 	})
539: 
540: 	return result

616: // getMonthlyStats retrieves monthly stats from tracker
617: func getMonthlyStats(tracker *tracking.Tracker) []MonthStats {
618: 	query := `
619: 		SELECT 
620: 			STRFTIME('%Y-%m', timestamp) as month,
621: 			COUNT(*) as commands,
622: 			COALESCE(SUM(saved_tokens), 0) as saved,
623: 			COALESCE(SUM(original_tokens), 0) as original
624: 		FROM commands
625: 		GROUP BY STRFTIME('%Y-%m', timestamp)
626: 		ORDER BY month DESC
627: 	`
628: 
629: 	rows, err := tracker.Query(query)
630: 	if err != nil {
631: 		return []MonthStats{}
632: 	}
633: 	defer rows.Close()
634: 
635: 	var stats []MonthStats
636: 	for rows.Next() {
637: 		var s MonthStats
638: 		var saved, original int
639: 		if err := rows.Scan(&s.Month, &s.Commands, &saved, &original); err != nil {
640: 			continue
641: 		}
642: 		s.SavedTokens = saved
643: 		if original > 0 {
644: 			s.SavingsPct = float64(saved) / float64(original) * 100
645: 		}
646: 		stats = append(stats, s)
647: 	}
648: 
649: 	return stats
650: }

724: // formatUSD formats a USD amount
725: func formatUSD(amount float64) string {
726: 	return fmt.Sprintf("$%.2f", amount)
727: }

729: // formatTokens formats a token count with K/M/B suffixes
730: func formatTokens(count uint64) string {
731: 	const billion = 1e9
732: 	const million = 1e6
733: 	const thousand = 1e3
734: 
735: 	if count >= billion {
736: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%.1fB", float64(count)/billion)
737: 	}
738: 	if count >= million {
739: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%.1fM", float64(count)/million)
740: 	}
741: 	if count >= thousand {
742: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%.1fK", float64(count)/thousand)
743: 	}
744: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%d", count)
745: }

747: // formatCPT formats cost-per-token
748: func formatCPT(cpt float64) string {
749: 	return fmt.Sprintf("$%.6f/tok", cpt)

Based on my deep analysis of the tokman codebase, here's a comprehensive summary of all RTK (Real-Time Kinematics) functionality found:

**RTK Features in TokMan:**

1. **Tokenizer** (`internal/tokenizer/tokenizer.go`)
   - Real-time token counting using tiktoken (OpenAI tokenizer)
   - Multiple encoding support: cl100k_base, o200k_base, p50k_base, r50k_base
   - Model-specific encodings for 30+ models (GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude, etc.)
   - Functions: `Count()`, `CountWithDetails()`, `CountFile()`, `CountReader()`, `CountMessages()`
   - Chat message token counting with proper formatting
   - `CompareCounts()` for heuristic vs actual token count comparison
   - `FormatCount()` for context-aware token display with model limits

2. **Filter Engine** (`internal/filter/filter.go`)
   - Real-time output filtering engine with multiple modes (none, minimal, aggressive)
   - 4 core filters: ANSI, Comment, Import, Log Aggregator
   - `Process()` applies filters sequentially with token savings tracking
   - Language detection (Go, Rust, Python, JS/TS, C, C++, Java, Ruby, Shell, SQL)
   - `CalculateTokensSaved()` computes savings between original and filtered output
   - `IsCode()` heuristic code detection

3. **ANSI Filter** (`internal/filter/ansi.go`)
   - Real-time ANSI escape sequence stripping
   - Patterns: OSC, CSI, SGR, link escape sequences
   - `StripANSI()`, `HasANSI()` utilities
   - Token savings calculation (bytes/4 heuristic)

4. **Log Aggregator** (`internal/filter/aggregator.go`)
   - Real-time log deduplication and compression
   - Timestamp stripping for comparison
   - Test result aggregation (PASS/FAIL summaries)
   - Build output condensation (warnings/errors)
   - `deduplicateLines()` with "(xN)" repetition marking
   - `GroupLines()` pattern-based grouping

5. **Import Filter** (`internal/filter/import.go`)
   - Language-specific import condensation (Go, Rust, Python, JS/TS)
   - Multi-line import block handling
   - Import count reporting with condensed markers

6. **Tee on Failure** (`internal/tee/tee.go`)
   - Real-time output capture and recovery
   - Configurable modes: never, failures only, always
   - File rotation with 20-file limit
   - Auto-saves full output when commands fail for debugging
   - Path hints with `~` expansion

7. **Tracking & Analytics** (`internal/tracking/tracker.go`)
   - SQLite database for real-time command tracking
   - Token metrics: original, filtered, saved
   - 90-day retention with automatic cleanup
   - Query methods: daily/weekly/monthly stats, command stats, parse failures
   - `Record()`, `RecordFallback()`, `RecordParseFailure()`
   - Savings percentage calculations

8. **Economics Analysis** (`internal/economics/economics.go`)
   - Real-time Claude Code spending vs savings
   - ccusage integration for token metrics
   - Weighted pricing: output=5x, cache_create=1.25x, cache_read=0.1x
   - Three pricing metrics: weighted input CPT, blended, active
   - Daily/weekly/monthly reports with export (JSON/CSV)
   - Dual-metric analysis combining tokman savings with ccusage costs

9. **Telemetry** (`internal/telemetry/telemetry.go`)
   - Anonymous telemetry pings every 23 hours
   - Device hash (SHA-256 of hostname:username)
   - Stats: commands per 24h, tokens saved, top commands
   - Opt-out via env var or config
   - Fire-and-forget HTTP POST via curl

10. **Direct Token Counting** (`internal/commands/count.go`)
    - `tokman count` command with tiktoken
    - Text, file, stdin, multiple file modes
    - Model-specific encodings: `--model gpt-4o`
    - Comparison mode: `--compare` for heuristic vs actual
    - Export formats: text, JSON, CSV

**Integration Points:**
- Shell hooks rewrite commands via `tokman-rewrite.sh`
- Dashboard provides web UI for analytics (`internal/dashboard/dashboard.go`)
- 40+ command handlers in `internal/commands/` for various CLI tools
- Plugin system for custom filter rules
I'll continue the deep dive into tokman's features, examining the remaining key components:

159: 			os.Exit(1)
160: 		}
161: 
162: 		fmt.Print(output)
163: 
164: 		// Record to tracker
165: 		if err := recordCommand("git log", output, output, execTime, true); err != nil && verbose > 0 {
166: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to record: %v\n", err)
167: 		}
168: 	},
169: }

216: // runGitStatus executes git status with porcelain parsing
217: func runGitStatus() (string, error) {
218: 	// Get porcelain output for parsing
219: 	porcelainCmd := buildGitCmd("status", "--porcelain", "-b")
220: 	var porcelainOut bytes.Buffer
221: 	porcelainCmd.Stdout = &porcelainOut
222: 	if err := porcelainCmd.Run(); err != nil {
223: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("git status failed: %w", err)
224: 	}
225: 
226: 	// Parse porcelain output
227: 	status := parsePorcelain(porcelainOut.String())
228: 
229: 	// Format output
230: 	return formatStatus(status), nil
231: }
232: 
233: // parsePorcelain parses git status --porcelain -b output
234: func parsePorcelain(output string) *GitStatus {
235: 	status := &GitStatus{}
236: 	lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(output), "\n")
237: 
238: 	for i, line := range lines {
239: 		if i == 0 {
240: 			// Branch line: "## main...origin/main [ahead 2, behind 1]"
241: 			status.Branch, status.Ahead, status.Behind = parseBranchLine(line)
242: 			continue
243: 		}
244: 
245: 		if len(line) < 4 {
246: 			continue
247: 		}
248: 
249: 		code := line[:2]
250: 		filePath := strings.TrimSpace(line[3:])
251: 
252: 		// Handle renamed files (old -> new)
253: 		if strings.Contains(filePath, " -> ") {
254: 			parts := strings.Split(filePath, " -> ")
255: 			if len(parts) == 2 {
256: 				filePath = parts[1] // Show new name
257: 			}
258: 		}
259: 
260: 		gitFile := GitFile{Path: filePath, Code: code}
261: 
262: 		switch {
263: 		case strings.Contains(code, "U"):
264: 			// Unmerged/conflicted
265: 			status.Conflicted = append(status.Conflicted, gitFile)
266: 		case code == "??":
267: 			// Untracked
268: 			status.Untracked = append(status.Untracked, gitFile)
269: 		case code == "!!":
270: 			// Ignored - skip
271: 		case code[0] != ' ' && code[0] != '?':
272: 			// Staged changes
273: 			status.Staged = append(status.Staged, gitFile)
274: 			if code[1] != ' ' {
275: 				// Also modified in working tree
276: 				status.Modified = append(status.Modified, gitFile)
277: 			}
278: 		case code[1] != ' ':
279: 			// Modified in working tree (not staged)
280: 			status.Modified = append(status.Modified, gitFile)
281: 		}
282: 	}
283: 
284: 	return status
285: }
286: 
287: // parseBranchLine extracts branch name and ahead/behind counts
288: func parseBranchLine(line string) (branch string, ahead, behind int) {
289: 	// Remove "## " prefix
290: 	line = strings.TrimPrefix(line, "## ")
291: 
292: 	// Check for detached HEAD
293: 	if strings.HasPrefix(line, "HEAD detached") {
294: 		return "HEAD detached", 0, 0
295: 	}
296: 
297: 	// Check for initial branch (no tracking)
298: 	if !strings.Contains(line, "...") {
299: 		// Check for ahead/behind without tracking
300: 		if strings.Contains(line, "[") {
301: 			parts := strings.SplitN(line, "[", 2)
302: 			branch = strings.TrimSpace(parts[0])
303: 			ahead, behind = parseAheadBehind(parts[1])
304: 		} else {
305: 			branch = strings.TrimSpace(line)
306: 		}
307: 		return branch, ahead, behind
308: 	}
309: 
310: 	// Normal branch with tracking: "main...origin/main [ahead 2, behind 1]"
311: 	parts := strings.SplitN(line, "...", 2)
312: 	branch = strings.Fields(parts[0])[0] // Handle "main (no branch)"
313: 
314: 	if len(parts) > 1 && strings.Contains(parts[1], "[") {
315: 		// Extract ahead/behind
316: 		abParts := strings.SplitN(parts[1], "[", 2)
317: 		ahead, behind = parseAheadBehind(abParts[1])
318: 	}
319: 
320: 	return branch, ahead, behind
321: }
322: 
323: // parseAheadBehind parses "ahead 2, behind 1]" format
324: func parseAheadBehind(s string) (ahead, behind int) {
325: 	// Remove trailing ]
326: 	s = strings.TrimSuffix(s, "]")
327: 
328: 	// Match ahead N
329: 	aheadRe := regexp.MustCompile(`ahead (\d+)`)
330: 	if matches := aheadRe.FindStringSubmatch(s); len(matches) > 1 {
331: 		ahead, _ = strconv.Atoi(matches[1])
332: 	}
333: 
334: 	// Match behind N
335: 	behindRe := regexp.MustCompile(`behind (\d+)`)
336: 	if matches := behindRe.FindStringSubmatch(s); len(matches) > 1 {
337: 		behind, _ = strconv.Atoi(matches[1])
338: 	}
339: 
340: 	return ahead, behind
341: }

343: // formatStatus formats git status for output
344: func formatStatus(status *GitStatus) string {
345: 	var buf strings.Builder
346: 
347: 	green := color.New(color.FgGreen).SprintFunc()
348: 	cyan := color.New(color.FgCyan).SprintFunc()
349: 	yellow := color.New(color.FgYellow).SprintFunc()
350: 	red := color.New(color.FgRed).SprintFunc()
351: 	bold := color.New(color.Bold).SprintFunc()
352: 
353: 	// Branch line
354: 	buf.WriteString(bold("📌 ") + cyan(status.Branch))
355: 	if status.Ahead > 0 || status.Behind > 0 {
356: 		buf.WriteString(" [")
357: 		if status.Ahead > 0 {
358: 			buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("ahead %d", status.Ahead))
359: 		}
360: 		if status.Behind > 0 {
361: 			if status.Ahead > 0 {
362: 				buf.WriteString(", ")
363: 			}
364: 			buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("behind %d", status.Behind))
365: 		}
366: 		buf.WriteString("]")
367: 	}
368: 	buf.WriteString("\n")
369: 
370: 	// Staged files
371: 	if len(status.Staged) > 0 {
372: 		buf.WriteString(green("\n✅ Staged:\n"))
373: 		for _, f := range status.Staged {
374: 			statusChar := getStatusCode(f.Code[0])
375: 			buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("   %s %s\n", statusChar, f.Path))
376: 		}
377: 	}
378: 
379: 	// Modified files
380: 	if len(status.Modified) > 0 {
381: 		buf.WriteString(yellow("\n📝 Modified:\n"))
382: 		for _, f := range status.Modified {
383: 			buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("   %s\n", f.Path))
384: 		}
385: 	}
386: 
387: 	// Untracked files
388: 	if len(status.Untracked) > 0 {
389: 		buf.WriteString(red("\n❓ Untracked:\n"))
390: 		// Limit display
391: 		maxShow := 10
392: 		for i, f := range status.Untracked {
393: 			if i >= maxShow {
394: 				buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("   ... and %d more\n", len(status.Untracked)-maxShow))
395: 				break
396: 			}
397: 			buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("   %s\n", f.Path))
398: 		}
399: 	}
400: 
401: 	// Conflicted files
402: 	if len(status.Conflicted) > 0 {
403: 		buf.WriteString(red("\n⚠️  Conflicted:\n"))
404: 		for _, f := range status.Conflicted {
405: 			buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("   %s\n", f.Path))
406: 		}
407: 	}
408: 
409: 	// Clean state
410: 	if len(status.Staged) == 0 && len(status.Modified) == 0 &&
411: 		len(status.Untracked) == 0 && len(status.Conflicted) == 0 {
412: 		buf.WriteString(green("\n✓ Clean working tree\n"))
413: 	}
414: 
415: 	return buf.String()
416: }
417: 
418: // getStatusCode returns a status character for staged changes
419: func getStatusCode(code byte) string {
420: 	switch code {
421: 	case 'M':
422: 		return "M" // Modified
423: 	case 'A':
424: 		return "A" // Added
425: 	case 'D':
426: 		return "D" // Deleted
427: 	case 'R':
428: 		return "R" // Renamed
429: 	case 'C':
430: 		return "C" // Copied
431: 	default:
432: 		return string(code)
433: 	}
434: }

436: // runGitDiff executes git diff with filtering
437: func runGitDiff(args []string) (string, error) {
438: 	// Run git diff --stat first for summary
439: 	statArgs := append([]string{"--stat"}, args...)
440: 	statCmd := buildGitCmd("diff", statArgs...)
441: 	var statOut bytes.Buffer
442: 	statCmd.Stdout = &statOut
443: 	statCmd.Run() // Ignore error, diff may have no changes
444: 
445: 	// Run git diff for content
446: 	diffArgs := append([]string{}, args...)
447: 	diffCmd := buildGitCmd("diff", diffArgs...)
448: 	var diffOut bytes.Buffer
449: 	diffCmd.Stdout = &diffOut
450: 	if err := diffCmd.Run(); err != nil {
451: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("git diff failed: %w", err)
452: 	}
453: 
454: 	// Filter the diff output
455: 	filtered := filterDiff(diffOut.String())
456: 
457: 	// Combine stat and filtered diff
458: 	if statOut.Len() > 0 {
459: 		return statOut.String() + "\n" + filtered, nil
460: 	}
461: 	return filtered, nil
462: }
463: 
464: // filterDiff filters diff hunks to max 30 lines
465: func filterDiff(diff string) string {
466: 	lines := strings.Split(diff, "\n")
467: 	var result []string
468: 	hunkLineCount := 0
469: 	inHunk := false
470: 	maxHunkLines := 30
471: 
472: 	for _, line := range lines {
473: 		// Check for hunk header
474: 		if strings.HasPrefix(line, "@@") {
475: 			inHunk = true
476: 			hunkLineCount = 0
477: 			result = append(result, line)
478: 			continue
479: 		}
480: 
481: 		// Check for file header
482: 		if strings.HasPrefix(line, "diff --git") ||
483: 			strings.HasPrefix(line, "index ") ||
484: 			strings.HasPrefix(line, "--- ") ||
485: 			strings.HasPrefix(line, "+++ ") {
486: 			inHunk = false
487: 			result = append(result, line)
488: 			continue
489: 		}
490: 
491: 		// Filter hunk content
492: 		if inHunk {
493: 			hunkLineCount++
494: 			if hunkLineCount <= maxHunkLines {
495: 				result = append(result, line)
496: 			} else if hunkLineCount == maxHunkLines+1 {
497: 				result = append(result, gray("... (hunk truncated)"))
498: 			}
499: 		} else {
500: 			result = append(result, line)
501: 		}
502: 	}
503: 
504: 	return strings.Join(result, "\n")
505: }
506: 
507: // gray returns a gray-colored string
508: func gray(s string) string {
509: 	dim := color.New(color.FgHiBlack).SprintFunc()
510: 	return dim(s)
511: }

513: // runGitLog executes git log with filtering
514: func runGitLog(args []string, fullOutput bool) (string, error) {
515: 	// Default args if none provided
516: 	logArgs := args
517: 	if len(logArgs) == 0 && !fullOutput {
518: 		// Use oneline format with count limit
519: 		logArgs = []string{"--oneline", "-n", "20"}
520: 	} else if len(logArgs) == 0 {
521: 		logArgs = []string{"-n", "20"}
522: 	}
523: 
524: 	cmd := buildGitCmd("log", logArgs...)
525: 	var out bytes.Buffer
526: 	cmd.Stdout = &out
527: 	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
528: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("git log failed: %w", err)
529: 	}
530: 
531: 	// Filter output
532: 	output := out.String()
533: 	if !fullOutput {
534: 		output = filterLog(output)
535: 	}
536: 
537: 	return output, nil
538: }

540: // filterLog filters git log output
541: func filterLog(output string) string {
542: 	// Strip ANSI codes
543: 	output = filter.StripANSI(output)
544: 
545: 	// Limit to reasonable size
546: 	lines := strings.Split(output, "\n")
547: 	if len(lines) > 50 {
548: 		lines = lines[:50]
549: 		lines = append(lines, gray("... (log truncated, use --verbose for full output)"))
550: 	}
551: 
552: 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
553: }

555: // recordCommand records a command execution to the tracker
556: func recordCommand(command, originalOutput, filteredOutput string, execTimeMs int64, success bool) error {
557: 	cfg, err := config.Load(cfgFile)
558: 	if err != nil {
559: 		return err
560: 	}
561: 
562: 	if !cfg.Tracking.Enabled {
563: 		return nil
564: 	}
565: 
566: 	tracker, err := tracking.NewTracker(cfg.GetDatabasePath())
567: 	if err != nil {
568: 		return err
569: 	}
570: 	defer tracker.Close()
571: 
572: 	originalTokens := tracking.EstimateTokens(originalOutput)
573: 	filteredTokens := tracking.EstimateTokens(filteredOutput)
574: 	savedTokens := 0
575: 	if originalTokens > filteredTokens {
576: 		savedTokens = originalTokens - filteredTokens
577: 	}
578: 
579: 	record := &tracking.CommandRecord{
580: 		Command:        command,
581: 		OriginalOutput: originalOutput,
582: 		FilteredOutput: filteredOutput,
583: 		OriginalTokens: originalTokens,
584: 		FilteredTokens: filteredTokens,
585: 		SavedTokens:    savedTokens,
586: 		ProjectPath:    config.ProjectPath(),
587: 		ExecTimeMs:     execTimeMs,
588: 		Timestamp:      time.Now(),
589: 		ParseSuccess:   success,
590: 	}
591: 
592: 	return tracker.Record(record)
593: }

645: 	statCmd := buildGitCmd("show", statArgs...)
646: 	var statOut bytes.Buffer
647: 	statCmd.Stdout = &statOut
648: 	statCmd.Run()
649: 
650: 	// Get diff
651: 	diffArgs := []string{"--pretty=format:"}
652: 	diffArgs = append(diffArgs, args...)
653: 	diffCmd := buildGitCmd("show", diffArgs...)
654: 	var diffOut bytes.Buffer
655: 	diffCmd.Stdout = &diffOut
656: 	diffCmd.Run()
657: 
658: 	var result strings.Builder
659: 	result.WriteString(summaryOut.String())
660: 	result.WriteString("\n")
661: 	if statOut.Len() > 0 {

663: 		result.WriteString("\n")
664: 	}
665: 	if diffOut.Len() > 0 {
666: 		result.WriteString("\n--- Changes ---\n")
667: 		result.WriteString(filterDiff(diffOut.String()))
668: 	}
669: 
670: 	return result.String(), nil
671: }

736: func runGitCommit(args []string) (string, error) {
737: 	commitCmd := buildGitCmd("commit", args...)
738: 	output, err := commitCmd.CombinedOutput()
739: 	outStr := string(output)
740: 
741: 	if err != nil {
742: 		if strings.Contains(outStr, "nothing to commit") {
743: 			return "ok (nothing to commit)", nil
744: 		}
745: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("git commit failed: %w\n%s", err, outStr)
746: 	}
747: 
748: 	// Extract commit hash from output like "[main abc1234] message"
749: 	for _, line := range strings.Split(outStr, "\n") {
750: 		if strings.HasPrefix(line, "[") {
751: 			// Parse "[branch hash] message"
752: 			parts := strings.Fields(line)
753: 			for _, p := range parts {
754: 				if len(p) >= 7 && isHexHash(p) {
755: 					return fmt.Sprintf("ok ✓ %s", p[:7]), nil
756: 				}
757: 			}
758: 		}
759: 	}
760: 
761: 	return "ok ✓", nil
762: }

764: func isHexHash(s string) bool {
765: 	if len(s) < 7 {
766: 		return false
767: 	}
768: 	for _, c := range s[:7] {
769: 		if !((c >= '0' && c <= '9') || (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f') || (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F')) {
770: 			return false
771: 		}
772: 	}
773: 	return true
774: }
